manCTMed 0.9.1
  • Get started

  • Articles
    Simulated Data Using Parameters from Deboeck and Preacher 2015 Continuous Time Mediation - Delta Method Continuous Time Mediation - Monte Carlo Method The Ornstein–Uhlenbeck Model manCTMed: Session
  • Changelog

Simulate Data

Source: R/manCTMed-data.R
Data.Rd

The function simulates data using the simStateSpace::SimSSMOUFixed() function.

Usage

Data(repid, n)

Arguments

repid

Positive integer. Replication ID.

n

Positive integer. Sample size.

See also

Other Simulation Functions: CI(), FitCtsem(), FitDynr(), Replication(), Summarize()

Examples

Data(repid = 1, n = 50)
#> $args
#> $args$repid
#> [1] 1
#> 
#> $args$n
#> [1] 50
#> 
#> $args$seed
#> [1] 1
#> 
#> $args$time
#> [1] 168
#> 
#> $args$delta_t
#> [1] 0.04166667
#> 
#> $args$k
#> [1] 3
#> 
#> $args$p
#> [1] 3
#> 
#> $args$mu0
#> [1] 0 0 0
#> 
#> $args$sigma0
#>      [,1] [,2] [,3]
#> [1,]  1.0  0.2  0.2
#> [2,]  0.2  1.0  0.2
#> [3,]  0.2  0.2  1.0
#> 
#> $args$sigma0_l
#>      [,1]      [,2]      [,3]
#> [1,]  1.0 0.0000000 0.0000000
#> [2,]  0.2 0.9797959 0.0000000
#> [3,]  0.2 0.1632993 0.9660918
#> 
#> $args$mu
#> [1] 0 0 0
#> 
#> $args$phi
#>        [,1]   [,2]   [,3]
#> [1,] -0.357  0.000  0.000
#> [2,]  0.771 -0.511  0.000
#> [3,] -0.450  0.729 -0.693
#> 
#> $args$sigma
#>             [,1]       [,2]        [,3]
#> [1,]  0.24455556 0.02201587 -0.05004762
#> [2,]  0.02201587 0.07067800  0.01539456
#> [3,] -0.05004762 0.01539456  0.07553061
#> 
#> $args$sigma_l
#>             [,1]      [,2]     [,3]
#> [1,]  0.49452559 0.0000000 0.000000
#> [2,]  0.04451917 0.2620993 0.000000
#> [3,] -0.10120330 0.0759256 0.243975
#> 
#> $args$nu
#> [1] 0 0 0
#> 
#> $args$lambda
#>      [,1] [,2] [,3]
#> [1,]    1    0    0
#> [2,]    0    1    0
#> [3,]    0    0    1
#> 
#> $args$theta
#>      [,1] [,2] [,3]
#> [1,]  0.2  0.0  0.0
#> [2,]  0.0  0.2  0.0
#> [3,]  0.0  0.0  0.2
#> 
#> $args$theta_l
#>           [,1]      [,2]      [,3]
#> [1,] 0.4472136 0.0000000 0.0000000
#> [2,] 0.0000000 0.4472136 0.0000000
#> [3,] 0.0000000 0.0000000 0.4472136
#> 
#> 
#> $sim
#> Call:
#> SimSSMFixed(n = n, time = time, delta_t = delta_t, mu0 = mu0, 
#>     sigma0_l = sigma0_l, alpha = ssm$alpha, beta = ssm$beta, 
#>     psi_l = ssm$psi_l, nu = nu, lambda = lambda, theta_l = theta_l, 
#>     type = type, x = x, gamma = gamma, kappa = kappa)
#> Use `as.data.frame` or `as.matrix` on the output of `SimSSMOUFixed`
#> to convert it to a data frame or a matrix.
#> 
#> 
#> $data
#>      id       time             x             m             y
#> 1     1 0.00000000 -1.4267592525 -0.9723918624 -5.630702e-02
#> 2     1 0.04166667 -0.6639975967 -1.4989179910  4.121500e-01
#> 3     1 0.08333333            NA            NA            NA
#> 4     1 0.12500000 -0.9736680705 -0.6979132869 -1.149200e-01
#> 5     1 0.16666667            NA            NA            NA
#> 6     1 0.20833333 -1.2899430445 -0.4826803595 -2.864957e-01
#> 7     1 0.25000000 -1.2127095471 -1.1325007558 -2.100445e-01
#> 8     1 0.29166667            NA            NA            NA
#> 9     1 0.33333333 -1.5609973013 -0.3003795061 -4.048406e-01
#> 10    1 0.37500000 -1.4292209154 -1.3862501743  1.507311e-01
#> 11    1 0.41666667            NA            NA            NA
#> 12    1 0.45833333 -0.7619360398 -1.0928060032 -1.025072e+00
